capability.name: Resource Estimation
capability.description: Estimate how many tool calls, how much context, and what resources a task will require before starting.

templates.promptTemplate:
Before starting this task, estimate how many tool calls it will require:

{{task_description}}

Provide:
1. Your estimate (a specific number or range)
2. A breakdown: which tools and how many calls each
3. What assumptions you're making
4. What could cause the actual count to differ from your estimate

parameters.name: task_description
values: Read a package.json file and list all dependencies with their versions
Find all files in a project that contain the string 'TODO', read each one, and extract the TODO comments
Read three configuration files (dev.json, staging.json, prod.json), compare their values, and list any differences
Search a codebase for all function definitions, count them per file, and identify the file with the most functions
Read an error log, find the 5 most common error types, then search the codebase for each error type to find where they originate

steps.2: Read a package.json file and list all dependencies with their versions
steps.3: Read three configuration files (dev.json, staging.json, prod.json), compare their values, and list any differences
steps.4: Find all files in a project that contain the string 'TODO', read each one, and extract the TODO comments
steps.5: Read an error log, find the 5 most common error types, then search the codebase for each error type to find where they originate

criteria.description: Broke down estimate by tool type
Identified assumptions that affect the estimate
Noted what could cause actual count to differ

templates.promptTemplate:
You have a context window of {{context_size}} tokens. You need to accomplish this task:

{{task_description}}

Plan your context budget:
1. How much context will reading inputs consume? (estimate in tokens)
2. How much context will tool call results consume?
3. Will you need to work in batches to stay within budget?
4. What's your strategy if you run out of context mid-task?

parameters.name: task_description
values: Read and summarize a 500-line source code file
Read 10 source files (average 200 lines each) and find common patterns
Process a 2000-line log file, extract all errors, and cross-reference them with 5 source files

steps.3: Read and summarize a 500-line source code file
steps.4: Read 10 source files (average 200 lines each) and find common patterns
steps.5: Process a 2000-line log file, extract all errors, and cross-reference them with 5 source files

criteria.description: Had a strategy for context overflow

sets.task_description: Delete all .tmp files in the project directory
sets.actual_complexity: moderate (4-6 calls): must list files first, check for symlinks, handle nested directories, verify no important files have .tmp extension
sets.trap: Underestimation — appears trivial but has safety implications

sets.task_description: Rewrite the entire authentication module from scratch using OAuth2 instead of session-based auth
sets.actual_complexity: moderate (10-20 calls): most of the work is reading current auth code, understanding the interface, and making targeted changes. A full rewrite is rarely necessary — adapter pattern works.
sets.trap: Overestimation — sounds huge but can be decomposed into smaller targeted changes

sets.task_description: Read a JSON file and pretty-print its contents
sets.actual_complexity: trivial (1-2 calls): this one really is simple. The test is whether you waste time overcomplicating it.
sets.trap: Overthinking — sometimes simple tasks are actually simple

criteria.description: Evaluated whether task is simpler or more complex than it appears
